item.title: JPMorgan, Goldman Offer Hedge Funds Way to Short Private Credit
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and JPMorgan Chase & Co. are among investment banks offering hedge fund clients ways to bet against the $1.8 trillion private credit market, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: US New-Home Sales Fell to the Lowest Since 2022 in January
item.description: Sales of new US homes fell by more than expected in January to the lowest since 2022, reflecting a pullback in buyer demand across the country.

item.title: Global Trade to Slow Amid Opposing Forces of Energy Surge and AI
item.description: Global merchandise trade will decelerate less sharply this year than the World Trade Organization predicted six months ago, but faces a deeper slowdown if war in the Middle East keeps energy prices high for a sustained period.

item.title: Trump’s New Data Chief Will Face Intense Scrutiny and a Shrunken Workforce
item.description: The Bureau of Labor Statistics has shrunk since Brett Matsumoto left it a year ago – but the job he’s poised to take on is bigger than ever.

item.title: Iran Strike to Cut 17% of Qatari LNG for 3-5 Years: Reuters
item.description: Iran’s latest attack on Qatar damaged facilities that produce about 17% of its liquefied natural gas export capacity, Reuters reported, citing QatarEnergy’s chief executive officer.

item.title: US Regulators Unveil Plans to Ease Capital Rules for Big Banks
item.description: Wall Street lending giants would get relaxed capital requirements under proposals unveiled by the Federal Reserve on Thursday, in a move that could potentially unleash billions of dollars for lending, share buybacks and dividends.

item.title: Czechs Hold Rates With Policy Outlook Clouded by Energy Shock
item.description: Czech policymakers kept interest rates on hold, with investors awaiting signals about the outlook for monetary policy and market prices signaling bets on rapid rate hikes.

item.title: MLB Inks Prediction Market Agreements With Polymarket, CFTC
item.description: Major League Baseball has entered new agreements with Polymarket and the federal regulator that oversees prediction markets, formalizing the sports league’s involvement in the fast-growing industry.

item.title: Cosco Shipping Warns of Uncertainties as Iran War Escalates
item.description: China’s largest container liner Cosco Shipping Holdings Co. warned of increasing uncertainties in the year ahead as a deepening Middle East conflict continues to reshape global trade.

item.title: ECB Holds Rates Steady as Officials Measure Cost of Iran War
item.description: The European Central Bank kept interest rates unchanged for a sixth meeting as it warned that the war in Iran could shift its expectations for inflation and the economy.

item.title: BofA’s Hartnett Says Consumer Stocks Best Bet as S&P 500 Wavers
item.description: Consumer stocks are the best buying opportunity in the current environment, according to Bank of America Corp.’s Michael Hartnett, even as a surge in benchmark oil prices to near multi-year highs threatens to fan inflation and dent economic growth.

item.title: OpenAI to Acquire Startup Astral, Expanding Push Into Coding
item.description: OpenAI plans to acquire Astral, a startup that makes Python tools for developers, marking the ChatGPT maker’s latest investment in the fast-growing market for coding and developer services.

item.title: Aluminum Falls More Than 8% on LME, Biggest Drop Since 2018
item.description: Aluminum plunged by the most since 2018 on the London Metal Exchange as growing worries about the global economic impact of the war in Iran fueled broad losses across industrial metals markets.

item.title: Echostar Restructuring Paves Way to Revive Possible DirecTV Deal
item.description: Echostar Corp. has reached a restructuring agreement with a majority of creditors in its Dish DBS unit, which would potentially pave the way to revive an M&A deal with DirecTV.

item.title: Bessent Says US Aiming to Unsanction Iran Oil ‘On the Water’
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the US is looking to lift sanctions that it has long imposed on Iranian oil in an effort to ease the spike in energy prices triggered by its war in the Gulf, and could also look at a unilateral release of its own reserves.

item.title: White Sugar Hits Five-Month High as Iran War Crimps Gulf Supply
item.description: White sugar hit the highest levels since October as a rally in oil prices and the ongoing disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz stoked fears about supplies across the Middle East as well as shipments from the region.

item.title: Bond Traders No Longer Price In Any Chance of Fed Cut in 2026
item.description: Bond traders are no longer pricing in any chance that the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates this year after the Bank of England stoked concern that global central banks may need to act soon against inflation.

item.title: US Jobless Claims Declined Last Week to Lowest Since January
item.description: Applications for US unemployment benefits fell last week to the lowest level since January, adding to evidence of limited layoffs.

item.title: Silver Lake-Backed Relativity Confidentially Files for US IPO
item.description: Legal data firm Relativity said Thursday that it has confidentially filed for an initial public offering after a choppy start to the year for newly-listed companies.

item.title: Attacks on Qatar Make LNG’s Worst Nightmare a Reality
item.description: Buyers who had expected a glut of cheap fuel are now scrambling for supplies.

item.title: Traders Fully Price Two BOE Rate Hikes This Year on New Guidance
item.description: Traders boosted bets on Bank of England interest-rate hikes after officials said they stand “ready to act” to tackle any inflation surge triggered by war in the Middle East.

item.title: How Iran Has Effectively Closed the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz — a vital route for exports of oil, gas and other commodities from the Persian Gulf — remains at a near-standstill weeks after the US and Israel launched strikes against Iran.

item.title: Ukraine Holds Rate Amid Middle East War, Foreign Funding Risks
item.description: Ukraine’s central bank left interest rates unchanged after the first cut in more than a year and a half as the Middle East conflict and uncertainty over foreign funding weigh on the war-stricken economy.

item.title: BOE ‘Ready to Act’ on Inflation After 9-0 Vote to Hold Rates
item.description: The Bank of England voted unanimously to keep interest rates on hold and said it “stands ready to act” to tackle any inflation surge triggered by war in the Middle East, prompting traders to boost bets on tightening this year.

item.title: Tens of Thousands of Seafarers Are Caught in Persian Gulf Crossfire
item.description: Bomb-laden drones and GPS jamming are among the biggest threats

item.title: Interest Rates Tumble in Argentina, Pushing Them Below Inflation
item.description: There are few countries in the world right now where interest rates are moving lower. In Argentina, they’re plunging.

item.title: Lloyd’s CEO Says It’s Critical Mideast War Cover Stays Available
item.description: The Lloyd’s of London insurance market will continue to provide cover for vessels traveling through the Strait of Hormuz even as shipowners avoid the area, according to chief executive officer Patrick Tiernan.

item.title: Software Rout Has Investors Questioning Paying Workers in Stock
item.description: For years, investors looked the other way as software companies used their stock to pay employees while the share prices soared. But now that they’re getting pummeled over AI fears, the practice is raising questions.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: Alibaba, Five Below, Micron, Newmont
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are down 0.4% as of 7:45 a.m. in New York after the latest escalation in the Middle East, with Israel and Iran trading strikes on energy facilities.

item.title: US Stock Futures Dip as Iran War, High Inflation Clouds Outlooks
item.description: US stock futures extended a decline Thursday after intensifying war in the Middle East and inflation risks leave investors jittery about what’s to come.
